title: Automated Futures Trading: How It Actually Runs
excerpt: How automated futures trading actually works: a rules engine placing ES orders, hard risk limits, and a kill switch. No passive income hype.

title: Risk Management in Futures: The Core Rules
excerpt: Risk management in futures trading means fixed per-trade risk, a daily loss cap, and sizing off notional value, not hope.

title: Systematic Trading: Rules Over Discretion
excerpt: Systematic trading replaces gut-feel decisions with fixed rules. What it is, how it differs from discretionary trading, and where the edge comes from.

title: Backtesting a Strategy Without Fooling Yourself
excerpt: What honest backtesting actually requires: real trading costs, out-of-sample data, and why almost any strategy looks good on paper.

title: Trading Psychology: Building Systems Over Willpower
excerpt: Trading psychology explains why losses hurt more than gains feel good. See why rules and a kill switch beat willpower under pressure.

title: What Is a Futures Contract? Plain Mechanics
excerpt: What is a futures contract? A plain explanation of the agreement, margin vs notional value, and why index futures like the ES settle in cash.

title: Futures Prop Firms: How the Model Works
excerpt: How a futures prop firm actually works: evaluation fees, funded accounts, profit splits, and the drawdown rules that trip up most traders.

title: Futures Trading Platforms Compared
excerpt: A plain comparison of futures trading platforms: NinjaTrader, Tradovate, and others on charting, DOM, automation, and cost.

title: Tradovate: Cloud Futures Trading Explained
excerpt: Tradovate explained: how its cloud platform works, its commission and subscription pricing, and how its DOM compares to desktop trading software.

title: NinjaTrader: Platform, Data, and Costs
excerpt: How NinjaTrader's charting, NinjaScript, data feeds, and FCM connections fit together for ES futures, and what each piece actually costs.

title: Futures vs Options: Risk, Margin, Payoff
excerpt: Futures vs options for ES trading: linear payoff vs premium decay, margin as a performance bond vs margin as sunk cost, and why directional traders often pick futures.

title: Paper Trading: What It Proves and What It Doesn't
excerpt: What paper trading proves, what it misses, and how long a forward test needs to run before it means anything.

title: Footprint Charts: Reading Bid-Ask Volume
excerpt: A footprint chart shows the volume traded at the bid and ask at every price level, so you can see order flow imbalances as they form.

title: VIX Futures: Trading Expected Volatility
excerpt: VIX futures explained: how they settle, why the curve is usually in contango, and why long VIX positions bleed money over time.

title: Is Trading Gambling? The Edge Test
excerpt: Is trading gambling? The real test isn't the market, it's expectancy math and whether you follow your rules. Here's how to check.

title: Apex Trader Funding: How Its Rules Work
excerpt: Apex Trader Funding explained: how the one-step evaluation, trailing threshold, and payout split actually work, in plain numbers.

title: Calmar Ratio: Return Against Max Drawdown
excerpt: Calmar ratio measures return against max drawdown, exposing strategies that look strong on CAGR alone. Formula, example, and limits explained.

title: Futures vs Forex: Fees, Regulation, Depth
excerpt: Futures vs forex compared: how CME futures fees, regulation, and market depth differ from decentralized spot forex trading.
